channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Judge denies Elon Musk's request to block OpenAI for-profit conversion but welcomes trial
item.description: A federal judge has denied Elon Musk’s request for a court order blocking OpenAI from converting itself to a for-profit company.
item.dc:creator: Matt O'Brien And Barbara Ortutay, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Elon Musk’s bumbling X posts are inadvertently teaching everyone how government works
item.description: If what you don’t know can’t hurt you, Elon Musk may be in luck. With a series of candid posts on X, the White House’s resident broligarch has lately been divulging which aspects of civics and data science he appears to know little about.
item.dc:creator: Joe Berkowitz, Fast Company

item.title: Georgia lawmakers push to ban automated speed cameras near schools
item.description: DECATUR, Ga. (AP) — Outside Beacon Hill Middle School in the Atlanta suburb of Decatur, like along hundreds of roadsides across Georgia, the unblinking eye of a camera tickets drivers who speed through a school zone.
item.dc:creator: JEFF AMY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Apple and Dassault Systèmes Team Up to Launch Vision Pro App - A New Era in 3D Designing
item.description: Apple and Dassault Systèmes have partnered to launch the Vision Pro app, bringing a new era of spatial computing and immersive experiences for designers, engineers, and businesses.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Samsung has launched a flexible portable monitor complete with a briefcase, one that seems to come straight from a James Bond movie
item.description: Samsung unveils a foldable OLED monitor that transforms into a briefcase, combining portability with high-resolution display technology.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Cybercrime crew stole then resold hundreds of tickets to Swift concerts, prosecutors say
item.description: A cybercrime crew stole then resold more than 900 digital tickets to Taylor Swift concerts and other pricey events.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Minnesota considers blocking 'nudify' apps that use AI to make explicit images without consent
item.description: ST. PAUL, Minn. (AP) — Molly Kelley was stunned to discover in June that someone she knew had used widely available “nudification” technology to create highly realistic and sexually explicit videos and images of her, using family photos that were posted on social media.
item.dc:creator: STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Shure's tiny MoveMic 88+ gives creators a cheap and easy way to record crystal clear audio on a smartphone
item.description: Shure MoveMic 88+ is a small, lightweight wireless mic designed for professional recordings via your smartphone
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: NASA's two stuck astronauts are finally closing in on their return to Earth after 9 months in space
item.description: NASA's two stuck astronauts are just a few weeks away from finally returning to Earth after nine months in space.
item.dc:creator: Marcia Dunn, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Netskope expands SASE footprint, bolsters AI and automation
item.description: Netskope is expanding its global presence by adding multiple regions to its NewEdge carrier-grade infrastructure, which now includes more than 75 locations to ensure processing remains close to end users.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: The world’s thinnest phone was just revealed, but a new iPhone 17 Air leak suggests it could be even slimmer
item.description: Tecno has revealed its new Spark Slim smartphone at MWC 2025, and at 5.75mm thin it’s got a solid claim to the title of the world’s thinnest slab phone – but a new iPhone 17 Air leak suggests it could be even thinner.
item.dc:creator: jamie.richards@futurenet.com (Jamie Richards), TechRadar

item.title: Uber teams up with Waymo to start selling driverless rides in Austin, Texas
item.description: Uber is shifting into a new gear in Austin, Texas where its ride-hailing service will begin dispatching self-driving cars to pick up passengers looking for a way to get around that city.
item.dc:creator: Michael Liedtke, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: DeepSeek claims 545% cost-profit ratio, challenging AI industry economics
item.description: Chinese AI startup DeepSeek has claimed its V3 and R1 models achieve a theoretical daily cost-profit ratio of 545%, highlighting cost implications for enterprises adopting similar models from other cloud providers.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Why genAI-powered intelligent document processing is a big deal
item.description: One of the earliest digital transformation drivers was digitalization, in which organizations converted paper-based processes to digitized workflows. In ideal circumstances, paper documents became web or mobile forms, and workflows replaced handoffs.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Hulu's Oscars Livestream Cut Off Abruptly: A Technical Fiasco
item.description: Hulu's live streaming of the 2024 Academy Awards ended on a chaotic note due to technical issues, causing viewers to miss pivotal moments of the show.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: MrBeast lost ‘tens of millions’ on his Amazon show. That’s a sign for other creators
item.description: Hollywood is waking up to the power of influencers. That starts with MrBeast. Amazon’s Beast Games cost hundreds of millions to produce. Some of that came from the streamer, but much of it came from the pockets of MrBeast, whose real name is Jimmy Donaldson.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: Tariffs an 'expensive game to play': Powerteam International CEO
item.description: Tariffs are going to be an "expensive game to play," Bill Walsh, CEO and founder of Powerteam International, said.
item.dc:creator: Connell McShane, NewsNation

item.title: Trump’s crypto reserve is a payoff for loyalists
item.description: President Donald Trump has found a new way to reward his supporters. On Sunday, Trump announced in a Truth Social post that he will issue an executive order creating a “Crypto Strategic Reserve” that will include Bitcoin, Ethereum, and three other cryptocurrencies. “I will make sure the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Scott Nover, Fast Company

item.title: EV-maker BYD seeks to raise $5.2 billion with share sale
item.description: Chinese electric vehicle maker BYD has launched a sale of its Hong Kong shares to raise up to $5.2 billion via an accelerated book-building, according to a deal term sheet seen by Reuters on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: TypeScript 5.8 reaches general availability
item.description: Microsoft’s TypeScript 5.8, the latest version of the company’s strongly typed superset of JavaScript, has reached general availability (GA).
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: AI Chatbots have telltale quirks. Researchers can spot them with 97% accuracy
item.description: There’s a cat-and-mouse game between those using generative AI chatbots to produce text undetected and those trying to catch them. Many believe they know the telltale signs—though as a journalist fond of the word “delve” and prone to em-dashes, I’m not so sure. Researchers at four U.S.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: Mozilla is under fire for its updated Firefox user agreement
item.description: Mozilla last week updated the Firefox user agreement — something that normally does not provoke strong reactions. This time, however, the changes led to a wave of criticism, because some of the wording can be interpreted as giving Mozilla free rein to do whatever it wants with users’ data.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: TSMC will invest $100B in US chip manufacturing
item.description: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSMC) plans to invest $100 billion to build chip manufacturing plants in the U.S., President Trump announced Monday. The multibillion-dollar investment, first reported by The Wall Street Journal, will stretch out over the next four years.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Americans split over Musk, federal workforce cuts: Survey
item.description: Americans in a new survey are split over their views of Elon Musk and his Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE), as well as federal workforce cuts during the opening weeks of the second Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Nvidia GTC 2025: What to expect from the AI leader
item.description: No company has both driven and benefited from AI advancements more than Nvidia. Last year, Nvidia’s GTC 2024 grabbed headlines with its introduction of the Blackwell architecture and the DGX systems powered by it. With GTC 2025 right around the corner (it runs March 17- 21 in San Jose, Calif.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: UK data protection watchdog investigating how TikTok uses children's personal data
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The U.K.'s data protection watchdog said Monday that it's investigating how TikTok uses the personal information of teenagers to deliver content recommendations to them when they use the social media platform.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIA HUI, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Everything you need to know about the Oscars’ AI controversy
item.description: Sometimes, authenticity can be a film’s most special effect. It took months for Best Actress front-runner Mikey Madison to learn how to pole dance like the titular exotic dancer in Anora and for her fellow nominee Timothée Chalamet to passably play guitar as Bob Dylan in A Complete Unknown.
item.dc:creator: Joe Berkowitz, Fast Company

item.title: Chip Ganassi Racing partners with OpenAI in first motorsports venture for AI company
item.description: ST. PETERSBURG, Fla. (AP) — Chip Ganassi Racing on Friday said it has partnered on a strategic research collaboration with OpenAI — a deal that makes the two-time reigning IndyCar championship winning team the first to bring in a partner from the artificial intelligence field.
item.dc:creator: JENNA FRYER, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Microsoft shutting down Skype in May
item.description: Microsoft is closing down Skype, the video-calling service it bought for $8.5 billion in 2011, which had helped spark a transformation in how people communicate online.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News
